name: Sunny Pasta Primavera
why: Light and fresh for sunny days
tip: Use fresh basil and high-quality olive oil
steps: Boil pasta according to package directions
Heat olive oil in a large pan
Sauté garlic until fragrant
Add fresh vegetables and cook until tender-crisp
Combine cooked pasta with vegetables
Season with salt and pepper and serve

name: Rainy Day Comfort Soup
why: Warm and comforting for rainy weather
steps: Heat broth in a large pot
Add chopped onion, carrots, and celery
Bring to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes
Add cooked chicken or beans
Add bay leaves and season to taste
Simmer for 15 more minutes and serve

name: Snowy Day Hot Chocolate
why: Warm treat for cold snowy days
tip: Top with marshmallows for extra fun
steps: Pour milk into a saucepan
Heat over medium heat (do not boil)
Mix cocoa powder and sugar in a bowl
Whisk cocoa mixture into hot milk
Add vanilla extract
Pour into mugs and top with marshmallows if desired

name: Windy Day Pizza
tip: Use pre-made dough to save time
steps: Preheat oven to 450°F
Stretch pizza dough on a baking sheet
Spread tomato sauce on dough
Add cheese and toppings
Bake for 12-15 minutes until crust is golden
Cut into slices and serve

name: Cloudy Day Pancakes
tip: Make them extra fluffy by not overmixing the batter
steps: Mix dry ingredients in a bowl
Whisk together milk, egg, and melted butter
Combine wet and dry ingredients (don't overmix)
Heat griddle or pan over medium-high heat
Pour batter and cook until bubbles form
Flip and cook until golden
Serve with syrup and toppings

name: Tropical Smoothie Bowl
tip: Use frozen fruit for a thicker consistency
steps: Blend frozen fruit with yogurt and milk until thick
Pour into bowls
Top with granola, fresh fruit, and coconut flakes
Serve immediately with a spoon

name: Cozy Bean Chili
why: Hearty and warming for cool days
tip: Let it simmer longer for deeper flavors
ingredients: Optional toppings: cheese, sour cream, green onions
steps: Heat oil in a large pot
Sauté onion and garlic
Add chili powder and cumin, stir for 1 minute
Add beans and tomatoes
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes
Season with salt and pepper
Serve with desired toppings

name: Garden Salad
why: Fresh and light for sunny days
tip: Dress just before serving to keep greens crisp
steps: Wash and dry the greens
Chop all vegetables
Combine greens and vegetables in a large bowl
Whisk together oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper
Dress salad just before serving
Top with nuts or seeds and serve

name: Baked Mac and Cheese
tip: Use a mix of cheeses for better flavor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F
Cook pasta until al dente
Make roux with butter and flour
Add milk and whisk until smooth
Stir in cheese and mustard
Combine pasta with sauce
Pour into baking dish, top with breadcrumbs
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden

name: Fish Tacos
why: Light and fun for any day
tip: Use fresh fish from the market if possible
steps: Season fish with salt, pepper, and spices
Pan-fry or grill fish until cooked through
Flake fish into bite-sized pieces
Warm tortillas or shells
Assemble tacos with fish and desired toppings
Serve with lime wedges and salsa
